2887    * For <b>host</b> contexts (top-level resources), {@link DefaultConfig} is always synthesized as the top-most
2888    * (parent-most) entry when not already present in the class hierarchy. This makes {@code DefaultConfig.@Rest(...)}
2889    * the framework's single source of truth for default lists (response processors, REST op args, encoders, parsers,
2890    * serializers, ...), while still letting bare {@code @Rest}-annotated resources inherit the framework defaults
2891    * without explicitly implementing {@link BasicUniversalConfig} or any other {@code DefaultConfig} descendant.
2892    * Resources that <i>do</i> implement {@code DefaultConfig} (transitively or directly) won't get a duplicate
2893    * entry — the list is dedup'd by the {@link Class} the annotation was declared on.
2894    *
2895    * <p>
2896    * For <b>mixin</b> sub-contexts the synthesized {@code DefaultConfig} fallback is omitted: the mixin's annotation
2897    * chain is composed with the host's chain by
2898    * {@link #getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String) getRestAnnotationsForProperty(...)}, and the host's chain already
2899    * supplies the {@code DefaultConfig} fallback (synthesized or transitive). Including it on the mixin too would
2900    * place a second {@code DefaultConfig} entry between the host's most-derived {@code @Rest} and the mixin's own
2901    * annotations in the combined parent-to-child walk, which would let the framework defaults silently override
2902    * a host's explicit per-property values for any property the mixin doesn't itself declare (e.g.
2903    * {@code partSerializer}, {@code partParser}).
2904    */
2905   private final Memoizer<List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>>> restAnnotations = memoizer(() -> prependBuilderRestAnnotation(computeRawRestAnnotations()));
2906
2907   /**
2908    * Computes the raw {@code @Rest} annotation chain (most-derived first), folding in the framework
2909    * {@code DefaultConfig} annotations for non-mixin contexts.  Does not include the synthetic builder annotation.
2910    *
2911    * @return The raw annotation chain.
2912    */
2913   private List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> computeRawRestAnnotations() {
2914      var raw = getAnnotationProvider().find(Rest.class, ClassInfo.of(getResourceClass()));
2915      if (isMixinContextField())
2916         return raw;
2917      var hasDefaultConfig = raw.stream().anyMatch(ai -> ai.getAnnotatable() instanceof ClassInfo ci && DefaultConfig.class.equals(ci.inner()));
2918      if (hasDefaultConfig)
2919         return raw;
2920      var defaultConfigAnnotations = getAnnotationProvider().find(Rest.class, ClassInfo.of(DefaultConfig.class));
2921      if (defaultConfigAnnotations.isEmpty())
2922         return raw;
2923      var combined = new ArrayList<>(raw);
2924      combined.addAll(defaultConfigAnnotations);
2925      return Collections.unmodifiableList(combined);
2926   }
2927
2928   /**
2929    * Prepends the synthetic builder-supplied {@code @Rest} annotation at the most-derived (child)
2930    * position so its set members win in both child-first ({@code findFirst}) and parent-to-child
2931    * ({@code reduce-last}) resolution walks.  Returns {@code base} unchanged when there is no programmatic builder.
2932    *
2933    * @param base The raw annotation chain.
2934    * @return The chain with the synthetic annotation prepended (when present).
2935    */
2936   private List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> prependBuilderRestAnnotation(List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> base) {
2937      if (builderRestAnnotation == null)
2938         return base;
2939      var combined = new ArrayList<AnnotationInfo<Rest>>(base.size() + 1);
2940      combined.add(AnnotationInfo.of(ClassInfo.of(getResourceClass()), builderRestAnnotation));
2941      combined.addAll(base);
2942      return Collections.unmodifiableList(combined);
2943   }
2944
2945   /**
2946    * Non-reflectively reads the programmatic configuration builder stashed on a resource/mixin instance,
2947    * or returns <jk>null</jk> when the instance carries none or is not a builder-aware base type.
2948    *
2949    * @param r The resource instance.
2950    * @return The stashed builder, or <jk>null</jk>.
2951    */
2952   private static RestBuilder<?> stashedRestBuilder(Object r) {
2953      if (r instanceof RestServlet x)
2954         return x.getRestBuilder();
2955      if (r instanceof RestResource x)
2956         return x.getRestBuilder();
2957      if (r instanceof RestMixin x)
2958         return x.getRestBuilder();
2959      return null;
2960   }
2961
2962   /**
2963    * The {@code @Rest} annotation list for this resource in parent-to-child (top-down) order.
2964    *
2965    * <p>
2966    * This is the reverse of {@link #restAnnotations}: most-ancestor annotation first, most-derived last.
2967    * Use this when walking the annotation chain in inheritance order so that child values append after or
2968    * override parent values.
2969    */
2970   private final Memoizer<List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>>> restAnnotationsTopDown = memoizer(() -> {
2971      var list = new ArrayList<>(getRestAnnotations());
2972      Collections.reverse(list);
2973      return Collections.unmodifiableList(list);
2974   });
2975
2976   private Stream<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> restAnnotationsForPropertySortedByRank(String propertyName) {
2977      return getRestAnnotationsForProperty(propertyName).sorted(Comparator.comparingInt(AnnotationInfo::getRank));
2978   }
2979
2980   private static String leadingEnumToken(String s) {
2981      s = trim(s);
2982      if (isEmpty(s))
2983         return s;
2984      int i = 0;
2985      while (i < s.length() && (Character.isLetter(s.charAt(i)) || s.charAt(i) == '_'))
2986         i++;
2987      return s.substring(0, i);
2988   }
2989
2990   private <E extends Enum<E>> Optional<E> parseEnumConstant(Class<E> enumClass, String resolved) {
2991      var t = leadingEnumToken(trim(emptyIfNull(resolved)));
2992      if (isEmpty(t))
2993         return opte();
2994      try {
2995         return opt(Enum.valueOf(enumClass, t));
2996      } catch (IllegalArgumentException ignored) {
2997         return opte();
2998      }
2999   }
3000
3001   String mergeReplacedStringAttribute(String propertyName, String initialFromEnvOrNull) {
3002      var v = new AtomicReference<>(initialFromEnvOrNull == null ? null : resolve(initialFromEnvOrNull));
3003      restAnnotationsForPropertySortedByRank(propertyName).forEach(ai ->
3004         ai.getString(propertyName)
3005            .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
3006            .map(this::resolve)
3007            .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
3008            .ifPresent(v::set));
3009      return v.get();
3010   }
3011
3012   private boolean mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(String propertyName, boolean envDefault) {
3013      var v = new AtomicReference<>(envDefault);
3014      restAnnotationsForPropertySortedByRank(propertyName).forEach(ai ->
3015         ai.getString(propertyName)
3016            .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
3017            .map(this::resolve)
3018            .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
3019            .ifPresent(s -> v.set(Boolean.parseBoolean(s))));
3020      return v.get();
3021   }
3022
3023   /**
3024    * Header names that may be passed via URL query parameter; resolved from {@code @Rest(allowedHeaderParams)},
3025    * default {@code "Accept,Content-Type"}.
3026    */
3027   private final Memoizer<Set<String>> allowedHeaderParams = memoizer(() ->
3028      Collections.unmodifiableSet(newCaseInsensitiveSet(mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_allowedHeaderParams, defaultAllowedHeaderParams))));
3029
3030   /**
3031    * HTTP method names that may be specified via a request header; resolved from {@code @Rest(allowedMethodHeaders)},
3032    * default empty.
3033    */
3034   private final Memoizer<Set<String>> allowedMethodHeaders = memoizer(() ->
3035      Collections.unmodifiableSet(newCaseInsensitiveSet(mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_allowedMethodHeaders, defaultAllowedMethodHeaders))));
3036
3037   /**
3038    * HTTP method names that may be specified via URL query parameter; resolved from {@code @Rest(allowedMethodParams)},
3039    * default {@code "HEAD,OPTIONS"}.
3040    */
3041   private final Memoizer<Set<String>> allowedMethodParams = memoizer(() ->
3042      Collections.unmodifiableSet(newCaseInsensitiveSet(mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_allowedMethodParams, defaultAllowedMethodParams))));
3043
3044   /**
3045    * Whether a {@code &content=} URL parameter may override the request body; inverse of
3046    * {@code @Rest(disableContentParam)}.
3047    */
3048   private final Memoizer<Boolean> allowContentParam = memoizer(() ->
3049      !mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_disableContentParam, defaultDisableContentParam));
3050
3051   /**
3052    * Whether exception stack traces are rendered in error responses; resolved from
3053    * {@code @Rest(renderResponseStackTraces)}.
3054    */
3055   private final Memoizer<Boolean> renderResponseStackTraces = memoizer(() ->
3056      mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_renderResponseStackTraces, defaultRenderResponseStackTraces));
3057
3058   /**
3059    * Whether the resource emits RFC 7807 {@code application/problem+json} responses; resolved from
3060    * {@code @Rest(problemDetails)}.
3061    */
3062   private final Memoizer<Boolean> problemDetails = memoizer(() ->
3063      mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_problemDetails, defaultProblemDetails));
3064
3065   /**
3066    * Whether the resource opts into per-request virtual-thread dispatch on Java 21+; resolved from
3067    * {@code @Rest(virtualThreads)}.
3068    *
3069    * <p>
3070    * Detection happens at context-init via {@link #mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(String, boolean)}; on JVMs older
3071    * than Java 21 the flag is logged once and ignored — see {@link #virtualThreadExecutor}.
3072    */
3073   private final Memoizer<Boolean> virtualThreadsEnabled = memoizer(() ->
3074      mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_virtualThreads, defaultVirtualThreads));
3075
3076   /**
3077    * Resource-level observability tri-state from {@code @Rest(observability)}: {@code "true"} (strict opt-in),
3078    * {@code "false"} (explicit opt-out), or {@code null} / empty (default inherit behavior).
3079    *
3080    * <p>
3081    * Per-operation overrides (from verb / {@code @RestOp} annotations) are resolved independently in
3082    * {@link RestOpContext}.
3083    */
3084   private final Memoizer<String> observabilityAttribute = memoizer(() ->
3085      mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_observability, null));
3086
3087   /**
3088    * Configurable async-response timeout (milliseconds) applied by {@code AsyncResponseProcessor} to
3089    * {@link CompletableFuture}-returning handlers; resolved from {@code @Rest(asyncTimeoutMillis)}.
3090    *
3091    * <p>
3092    * {@code 0} disables the timeout. The default 30-second fallback is applied by {@code AsyncResponseProcessor}
3093    * itself when neither the resource nor the operation declares a value.
3094    */
3095   private final Memoizer<Long> asyncTimeoutMillis = memoizer(() -> {
3096      var s = mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_asyncTimeoutMillis, null);
3097      if (isEmpty(s))
3098         return -1L;
3099      try {
3100         return Long.parseLong(s.trim());
3101      } catch (NumberFormatException nfe) {
3102         ASYNC_LOG.log(Level.WARNING, () -> "Invalid @Rest(asyncTimeoutMillis) value '" + s + "' — falling back to default.");
3103         return -1L;
3104      }
3105   });
3106
3107   /**
3108    * The async-completion executor for this resource, resolved by name from the bean store; {@code null} when
3109    * {@code @Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor)} is blank (the common case).
3110    *
3111    * <p>
3112    * The memoizer throws {@link IllegalStateException} at first access if the bean name was supplied but no
3113    * matching {@link Executor} bean is found — this surfaces as a startup failure, not a silent no-op.
3114    */
3115   private final Memoizer<Executor> asyncCompletionExecutor = memoizer(() -> {
3116      // Programmatic override takes priority over the annotation chain.
3117      var name = resolveAsyncCompletionExecutorName();
3118      if (isBlank(name))
3119         return null;
3120      var resolved = beanStore().getBean(Executor.class, name);
3121      // Try ExecutorService as well (a common supertype users supply that is-a Executor).
3122      if (resolved.isEmpty())
3123         resolved = beanStore().getBean(ExecutorService.class, name).map(e -> (Executor) e);
3124      if (resolved.isEmpty())
3125         throw new IllegalStateException(
3126            "@Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor) on " + getResourceClass().getName()
3127               + " references bean name '" + name + "' but no Executor (or ExecutorService) bean"
3128               + " with that name is registered in the resource's bean store.");
3129      return resolved.get();
3130   });
3131
3132   // Extracted to a method so the compiler does not flag a blank-final-field access inside a field-initializer lambda.
3133   private String resolveAsyncCompletionExecutorName() {
3134      return builder.asyncCompletionExecutorName != null
3135         ? builder.asyncCompletionExecutorName
3136         : mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_asyncCompletionExecutor, null);
3137   }
3138
3139   /**
3140    * Lazily-instantiated virtual-thread executor used by {@link RestOpInvoker} when
3141    * {@code @Rest(virtualThreads=true)} is set on this resource and the runtime is Java 21+.
3142    *
3143    * <p>
3144    * On Java 17/18/19/20 the supplier returns {@code null} and emits a one-shot {@code WARNING} log so the
3145    * resource degrades gracefully to caller-thread dispatch.
3146    */
3147   private final Memoizer<Executor> virtualThreadExecutor = memoizer(() -> {
3148      // NOTE: Intentionally not gated on resource-level {@code virtualThreadsEnabled.get()} — per-op
3149      // {@code @RestOp(virtualThreads="true")} can opt in even when the enclosing {@code @Rest} doesn't.
3150      // Callers ({@link RestOpInvoker#invokeOp}) only reach this method when the effective op-level flag is true,
3151      // so the warning emitted on Java &lt;21 is appropriate at first call regardless of where the flag is set.
3152      if (Runtime.version().feature() < 21) {
3153         ASYNC_LOG.log(Level.WARNING, () -> "virtualThreads=true configured on " + getResourceClass().getName()
3154            + " but runtime is Java " + Runtime.version().feature() + " — virtual-thread dispatch requires Java 21+. "
3155            + "Falling back to caller-thread dispatch.");
3156         return null;
3157      }
3158      try {
3159         var m = Executors.class.getMethod("newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor");
3160         return (Executor) m.invoke(null);
3161      } catch (ReflectiveOperationException e) {
3162         ASYNC_LOG.log(Level.WARNING, e, () -> "Reflective creation of virtual-thread executor failed on "
3163            + getResourceClass().getName() + " — falling back to caller-thread dispatch.");
3164         return null;
3165      }
3166   });
3167
3168   /** Logger for async / virtual-thread setup events. Used at memoizer init before {@link #getLogger()} may be wired up. */
3169   private static final Logger ASYNC_LOG = Logger.getLogger(RestContext.class.getName() + ".async");
3170
3171   /**
3172    * Whether framework memoizers and operation/child contexts should be force-initialized during constructor execution;
3173    * resolved from {@code @Rest(eagerInit)}.
3174    */
3175   private final Memoizer<Boolean> eagerInit = memoizer(() ->
3176      mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_eagerInit, defaultEagerInit));
3177
3178   /**
3179    * Annotation + env-driven component of the lazy-children flag.
3180    *
3181    * <p>
3182    * Reads the {@link Rest#lazyChildren() @Rest(lazyChildren)} annotation chain and falls back to the
3183    * {@code RestContext.lazyChildren} env-driven default.  The programmatic
3184    * {@link Builder#lazyChildInit(boolean)} knob is applied in {@link #isLazyChildren()} instead of here
3185    * because blank-final-field rules prevent the memoizer lambda from safely capturing {@link #builder}.
3186    */
3187   private final Memoizer<Boolean> lazyChildrenAnnotation = memoizer(() ->
3188      mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_lazyChildren, defaultLazyChildren));
3189
3190   /**
3191    * The request header used for client-version matching; resolved from {@code @Rest(clientVersionHeader)},
3192    * default {@code "Client-Version"}.
3193    */
3194   private final Memoizer<String> clientVersionHeader = memoizer(() ->
3195      mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_clientVersionHeader, defaultClientVersionHeader));
3196
3197   /**
3198    * The {@link UriRelativity} strategy for URI resolution in this resource.
3199    *
3200    * <p>
3201    * Resolved from {@code @Rest(uriRelativity)} annotations (most-derived non-blank wins), with env-var
3202    * fallback. Defaults to {@link UriRelativity#RESOURCE}.
3203    */
3204   private final Memoizer<UriRelativity> uriRelativity = memoizer(() -> {
3205      var v = new AtomicReference<>(
3206         parseEnumConstant(UriRelativity.class, resolve(emptyIfNull(defaultUriRelativity)))
3207            .orElse(UriRelativity.RESOURCE)
3208      );
3209      restAnnotationsForPropertySortedByRank(PROPERTY_uriRelativity).forEach(ai -> ai.getString(PROPERTY_uriRelativity).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).ifPresent(s ->
3210         parseEnumConstant(UriRelativity.class, resolve(s)).ifPresent(v::set)
3211      ));
3212      return v.get();
3213   });
3214
3215   /**
3216    * The URI authority (scheme + host + port) override for this resource.
3217    *
3218    * <p>
3219    * Resolved from {@code @Rest(uriAuthority)} annotations and env-var; inherited from parent when not
3220    * blocked by {@code noInherit}. {@code null} means no override.
3221    */
3222   private final Memoizer<String> uriAuthority = memoizer(() -> {
3223      String local = mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_uriAuthority, defaultUriAuthority.orElse(null));
3224      if (nn(local))
3225         return local;
3226      var pc = parentContext();
3227      return isInherited(PROPERTY_uriAuthority) && pc != null ? pc.getUriAuthority() : null;
3228   });
3229
3230   /**
3231    * The URI context path override for this resource.
3232    *
3233    * <p>
3234    * Resolved from {@code @Rest(uriContext)} annotations and env-var; inherited from parent when not
3235    * blocked by {@code noInherit}. {@code null} means no override.
3236    */
3237   private final Memoizer<String> uriContext = memoizer(() -> {
3238      String local = mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_uriContext, defaultUriContext.orElse(null));
3239      if (nn(local))
3240         return local;
3241      var pc = parentContext();
3242      return isInherited(PROPERTY_uriContext) && pc != null ? pc.getUriContext() : null;
3243   });
3244
3245   /**
3246    * The {@link UriResolution} strategy for URI resolution in this resource.
3247    *
3248    * <p>
3249    * Resolved from {@code @Rest(uriResolution)} annotations (most-derived non-blank wins), with env-var
3250    * fallback. Defaults to {@link UriResolution#ROOT_RELATIVE}.
3251    */
3252   private final Memoizer<UriResolution> uriResolution = memoizer(() -> {
3253      var v = new AtomicReference<>(
3254         parseEnumConstant(UriResolution.class, resolve(emptyIfNull(defaultUriResolution)))
3255            .orElse(UriResolution.ROOT_RELATIVE)
3256      );
3257      restAnnotationsForPropertySortedByRank(PROPERTY_uriResolution).forEach(ai -> ai.getString(PROPERTY_uriResolution).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).ifPresent(s ->
3258         parseEnumConstant(UriResolution.class, resolve(s)).ifPresent(v::set)
3259      ));
3260      return v.get();
3261   });
3262
3263   /**
3264    * Property names that describe how a class is mounted on the URL tree, and therefore must NOT inherit from a
3265    * parent context when walking annotations on a {@linkplain #isMixinContext() mixin sub-context}.
3266    *
3267    * <p>
3268    * These properties are intentionally host-only:
3269    * <ul>
3270    *    <li>{@code path} / {@code paths} &mdash; top-level mount declarations only meaningful for the class registered
3271    *       with the servlet container.  A mixin sub-context inheriting the host's mount paths could cause
3272    *       nonsensical routing decisions.
3273    *    <li>{@code mixins} &mdash; the host owns the mixin discovery chain; a mixin sub-context inheriting the host's
3274    *       mixin list would loop or duplicate route registration.
3275    *    <li>{@code children} &mdash; child sub-resources are tied to the host's URL namespace, not the mixin's.
3276    * </ul>
3277    *
3278    * <p>
3279    * The host's {@link RestContext} reads {@code path}/{@code paths} from its own annotation chain only (via
3280    * {@link #getRestAnnotations()} rather than {@link #getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String)}); this allowlist
3281    * preserves that invariant when a mixin sub-context walks annotations for these properties.
3282    */
3283   private static final Set<String> HOST_ONLY_PROPERTIES = Set.of(
3284      PROPERTY_path, PROPERTY_paths, PROPERTY_mixins, "children"
3285   );
3286
3287   /**
3288    * Returns the {@link Rest} annotations on the resource class hierarchy for the specified property,
3289    * in <b>parent-to-child</b> order, with {@code noInherit} cutoff applied.
3290    *
3291    * <p>
3292    * Used by both this class and {@link RestOpContext} to walk the class-level annotation chain when
3293    * computing memoized op-level settings that inherit from class-level {@code @Rest(...)} attributes.
3294    *
3295    * <p>
3296    * When this context is a {@linkplain #isMixinContext() mixin sub-context}, the parent context's annotation
3297    * chain is prepended to the mixin's own &mdash; serializers, parsers, encoders, converters, response
3298    * processors, guards, callLogger, debugEnablement, messages, and other contribution lists inherit from the
3299    * host first, with the mixin's contributions appended.  The local {@code @Rest(noInherit)} on the mixin
3300    * cuts off the parent walk for any specific property; the {@link #HOST_ONLY_PROPERTIES} allowlist
3301    * unconditionally skips the parent walk for properties whose semantics are host-only (mount paths, the
3302    * mixin discovery chain, and child sub-resources).
3303    *
3304    * @param name The annotation property name (e.g. {@code "converters"}, {@code "guards"}).
3305    * @return A stream of {@link AnnotationInfo} entries in parent-to-child order, never {@code null}.
3306    */
3307   Stream<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String name) {
3308      var localAnnotations = getRestAnnotations();
3309      List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> annotations;
3310      if (isMixinContextField() && parentContext != null && !HOST_ONLY_PROPERTIES.contains(name) && !noInherit.get().contains(name)) {
3311         var combined = new ArrayList<AnnotationInfo<Rest>>(localAnnotations.size() + parentContext.getRestAnnotations().size());
3312         // Local (child-to-parent) annotations first; then parent's (child-to-parent).
3313         // Since the final stream is reversed by rstream(), the combined order becomes:
3314         //   parent's parent-to-child, then mixin's parent-to-child — i.e. host annotations come first, then mixin.
3315         combined.addAll(localAnnotations);
3316         combined.addAll(parentContext.getRestAnnotations());
3317         annotations = combined;
3318      } else {
3319         annotations = localAnnotations;
3320      }
3321      var cutoff = annotations.size();
3322      for (var i = 0; i < annotations.size(); i++) {
3323         if (resolveCdl(annotations.get(i).getStringArray(PROPERTY_noInherit)).anyMatch(name::equalsIgnoreCase)) {
3324            cutoff = i + 1;
3325            break;
3326         }
3327      }
3328      var resolved = annotations.subList(0, cutoff);
3329      // Mixin override injection (cleanup #3): for a mixin sub-context carrying host @Mixin overrides, the
3330      // synthetic override annotation is the most-derived contribution.  `resolved` is in child-to-parent order
3331      // (it is reversed to parent-to-child by rstream below), so prepending here places the override LAST in the
3332      // emitted stream — list-shaped props append it after the inherited host+mixin chain, replace-shaped props
3333      // take it as the final (winning) value.  It is added after the noInherit cutoff scan so it is never itself
3334      // cut, and HOST_ONLY_PROPERTIES are excluded (path/paths/mixins/children are not @Mixin override slots).
3335      var override = HOST_ONLY_PROPERTIES.contains(name) ? null : mixinOverrideAnnotation.get();
3336      if (override == null)
3337         return rstream(resolved);
3338      var withOverride = new ArrayList<AnnotationInfo<Rest>>(resolved.size() + 1);
3339      withOverride.add(override);
3340      withOverride.addAll(resolved);
3341      return rstream(withOverride);
3342   }
3343
3344   /**
3345    * For a {@linkplain #isMixinContext() mixin sub-context}, returns the <b>host</b> resource class's
3346    * class-level annotation infos (host class chain, in parent-to-child order) so a mixin operation can
3347    * inherit the host's class-level {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.ContextApply @ContextApply} config
3348    * (e.g. {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.html.HtmlDocConfig @HtmlDocConfig},
3349    * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerConfig @SerializerConfig}).
3350    *
3351    * <p>
3352    * Used by {@link RestOpContext.Builder} to prepend the host's class-level config annotations <i>ahead of</i>
3353    * the mixin class's own class annotations in the op's annotation work-list, so the effective precedence is
3354    * method &gt; mixin-class &gt; host-class.  This is the page-decoration counterpart of the context-level
3355    * inheritance walk in {@link #getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String)}: that walk inherits {@code @Rest}
3356    * properties (serializers, parsers, guards, ...) from the host, while this method inherits standalone
3357    * class-level config annotations that are not {@code @Rest} attributes.
3358    *
3359    * <p>
3360    * Inheritance is automatic for every mixin sub-context.  A mixin can opt out of inheriting a specific host
3361    * config annotation by naming its annotation type in {@link Rest#noInherit() @Rest(noInherit)} on the mixin
3362    * class (e.g. {@code @Rest(noInherit={"HtmlDocConfig"})}), reusing the same {@code noInherit} resolution that
3363    * gates the context-level walk so the opt-out is uniform.  For non-mixin contexts (top-level resources and
3364    * child resources) this returns an empty stream &mdash; only mixin ops inherit from a composition host.
3365    *
3366    * @param ap The annotation provider to resolve the host class chain with.
3367    * @return The host class chain's annotation infos in parent-to-child order, or an empty stream when this is
3368    *    not a mixin sub-context (or has no parent).
3369    */
3370   Stream<AnnotationInfo<?>> getInheritedHostClassAnnotations(AnnotationProvider ap) {
3371      if (! isMixinContextField() || parentContext == null)
3372         return Stream.empty();
3373      var blocked = noInherit.get();
3374      return rstream(ap.find(ClassInfo.of(parentContext.getResourceClass()), SELF, PARENTS))
3375         .filter(ai -> ! blocked.contains(ai.annotationType().getSimpleName()));
3376   }
3377
3378   /**
3379    * Returns all {@link Rest} annotations on the resource class hierarchy, in child-to-parent order.
3380    *
3381    * @return An unmodifiable list of {@link AnnotationInfo} for {@link Rest}, never {@code null}.
3382    */
3383   protected List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> getRestAnnotations() {
3384      return restAnnotations.get();
3385   }
3386
3387   /**
3388    * Returns the nearest {@link Rest} annotation on this resource.
3389    *
3390    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the first (most-derived) {@link Rest} {@link AnnotationInfo}.
3391    */
3392   protected Optional<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> getRestAnnotation() {
3393      return getRestAnnotations().stream().findFirst();
3394   }
3395
3396   /**
3397    * Returns the {@link Rest} annotations for this resource in parent-to-child (top-down) order.
3398    *
3399    * <p>
3400    * This is the reverse of {@link #getRestAnnotations()}: most-ancestor annotation first,
3401    * most-derived last. Use this when annotation values should accumulate in inheritance order
3402    * (e.g. parent values are applied first and child values append or override them).
3403    *
3404    * @return An unmodifiable list of {@link AnnotationInfo} for {@link Rest}, never {@code null}.
3405    */
3406   protected List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> getRestAnnotationsTopDown() {
3407      return restAnnotationsTopDown.get();
3408   }
3409
3410   /**
3411    * Returns {@code true} if values for the given annotation attribute may be inherited from {@link #parentContext}.
3412    *
3413    * <p>
3414    * Inheritance is blocked when the nearest {@code @Rest(noInherit)} lists the property name (case-insensitive).
3415    *
3416    * @param property The annotation attribute name (e.g. {@code "allowedSerializerOptions"}).
3417    * @return {@code true} if parent values should be included.
3418    */
3419   protected boolean isInherited(String property) {
3420      return RestContext.this.parentContext != null && !noInherit.get().contains(property);
3421   }
3422
3423   /**
3424    * Returns {@code true} if the local {@code @Rest(noInherit=...)} list contains {@code property} as a literal token.
3425    *
3426    * <p>
3427    * Unlike the {@link #noInherit} memoizer this performs a raw, SVL-free, comma-split check on the local
3428    * {@code @Rest} annotation. It exists for callers that are themselves on the dependency chain of the
3429    * {@code varResolver} memoizer (e.g. the {@code messages} memoizer) — those callers can't reach the
3430    * regular {@link #noInherit} memoizer without inducing a cycle, because the regular path runs
3431    * {@code resolveCdl(...)} → {@link #getVarResolver()} → {@link #getMessages()} → back to messages.
3432    *
3433    * @param property The property name (e.g. {@code "messages"}). Case-insensitive comparison.
3434    * @return {@code true} if {@code property} is named as a literal token in the local {@code @Rest(noInherit=...)}.
3435    */
3436   private boolean isNoInheritLiteral(String property) {
3437      var fromRest = getRestAnnotation()
3438         .map(x -> x.getStringArray("noInherit").orElse(StringUtils.EMPTY_STRING_ARRAY))
3439         .stream()
3440         .flatMap(Arrays::stream)
3441         .filter(Objects::nonNull)
3442         .flatMap(s -> StringUtils.split(s, ',').stream())
3443         .map(String::trim)
3444         .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
3445         .anyMatch(property::equalsIgnoreCase);
3446      if (fromRest)
3447         return true;
3448      // Also honor a host @Mixin(noInherit=...) token (raw / SVL-free, same as the @Rest path above) so the
3449      // messages memoizer's cutoff check sees both noInherit sources without inducing the resolver cycle.
3450      if (resolvedMixin != null) {
3451         for (var s : resolvedMixin.overrides().noInherit())
3452            for (var t : StringUtils.split(s, ','))
3453               if (property.equalsIgnoreCase(t.trim()))
3454                  return true;
3455      }
3456      return false;
3457   }
3458
3459   /**
3460    * Resolves comma-delimited annotation values with SVL variable substitution.
3461    *
3462    * @param values Raw annotation attribute values.
3463    * @return A stream of trimmed, non-blank tokens.
3464    */
3465   private Stream<String> resolveCdl(String...values) {
3466      if (values == null || values.length == 0)
3467         return Stream.empty();
3468      return Arrays.stream(values)
3469         .filter(Objects::nonNull)
3470         .map(this::resolve)
3471         .map(StringUtils::split)
3472         .flatMap(Collection::stream)
3473         .map(String::trim)
3474         .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank);
3475   }
3476
3477   private Stream<String> resolveCdl(Optional<String[]> values) {
3478      return values.isEmpty() ? Stream.empty() : resolveCdl(values.get());
3479   }
3480
3481   /**
3482    * Resolves SVL variables in the given string.
3483    *
3484    * @param s The raw string. Can be {@code null}.
3485    * @return The resolved string.
3486    */
3487   protected String resolve(String s) {
3488      return getVarResolver().resolve(s);
3489   }
3490
3491   /**
3492    * Returns the parser session-option keys allowed for this resource.
3493    *
3494    * @return An unmodifiable case-insensitive sorted set, never {@code null}.
3495    */
3496   public SortedSet<String> getAllowedParserOptions() {
3497      return allowedParserOptions.get();
3498   }
3499
3500   /**
3501    * Returns the serializer session-option keys allowed for this resource.
3502    *
3503    * @return An unmodifiable case-insensitive sorted set, never {@code null}.
3504    */
3505   public SortedSet<String> getAllowedSerializerOptions() {
3506      return allowedSerializerOptions.get();
3507   }
3508
3509   /**
3510    * Called during servlet destruction to invoke all {@link RestDestroy} methods.
3511    *
3512    * <p>
3513    * Fires this context's own {@code @RestDestroy} methods first, then walks any mixin sub-contexts
3514    * (host destroys before each mixin's), then any {@code @Rest(children=...)} sub-resources, then closes
3515    * the bean store. Mixin sub-contexts recursively destroy themselves via the same path; flat-inheritance
3516    * guarantees no further mixin descent occurs from inside a mixin sub-context.
3517    */
3518   public void destroy() {
3519      for (var x : destroyInvokerPair.get().invokers) {
3520         try {
3521            x.invoke(beanStore, getResource());
3522         } catch (Exception e) {
3523            getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, unwrap(e), () -> String.format("Error occurred invoking servlet-destroy method '%s'.", x.getFullName()));
3524         }
3525      }
3526      if (!isMixinContext) {
3527         for (var mctx : mixinContexts.get().values()) {
3528            try {
3529               mctx.destroy();
3530            } catch (Exception e) {
3531               getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, unwrap(e), () -> String.format("Error occurred destroying mixin sub-context '%s'.", mctx.getResourceClass().getName()));
3532            }
3533         }
3534      }
3535      var childrenRef = getRestChildren();
3536      if (nn(childrenRef))
3537         childrenRef.destroy();
3538      try {
3539         beanStore.close();
3540      } catch (Exception e) {
3541         getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, unwrap(e), () -> "Error occurred closing bean store.");
3542      }
3543   }
3544
3545   /**
3546    * The main service method.
3547    *
3548    * <p>
3549    * Subclasses can optionally override this method if they want to tailor the behavior of requests.
3550    *
3551    * @param resource
3552    *    The REST servlet or bean that this context defines.
3553    *    <br>Note that this bean may not be the same bean used during initialization as it may have been replaced at runtime.
3554    * @param r1 The incoming HTTP servlet request object.
3555    * @param r2 The incoming HTTP servlet response object.
3556    * @throws ServletException General servlet exception.
3557    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
3558    */
3559   @SuppressWarnings({
3560      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for request execution logic
3561   })
3562   public void execute(Object resource, HttpServletRequest r1, HttpServletResponse r2) throws ServletException, IOException {
3563
3564      // Must be careful not to bleed thread-locals.
3565      if (nn(localSession.get()))
3566         LOG.warning("WARNING:  Thread-local call object was not cleaned up from previous request.  {}, thread=[{}]", this, Thread.currentThread().getName());
3567
3568      RestSession.Builder sb = createSession().resource(resource).req(r1).res(r2).logger(getCallLogger());
3569
3570      try {
3571
3572         if (nn(initException))
3573            throw initException;
3574
3575         // If the resource path contains variables (e.g. @Rest(path="/f/{a}/{b}"), then we want to resolve
3576         // those variables and push the servletPath to include the resolved variables.  The new pathInfo will be
3577         // the remainder after the new servletPath.
3578         // Only do this for the top-level resource because the logic for child resources are processed next.
3579         if (pathMatcher.hasVars() && parentContext == null) {
3580            var sp = sb.req().getServletPath();
3581            var pi = sb.getPathInfoUndecoded();
3582            var upi2 = UrlPath.of(pi == null ? sp : sp + pi);
3583            var uppm = pathMatcher.match(upi2);
3584         if (nn(uppm) && ! uppm.hasEmptyVars()) {
3585            sb.pathVars(uppm.getVars());
3586            var pathInfo = opt(validatePathInfo(nullIfEmpty(urlDecode(uppm.getSuffix())))).orElse("\u0000");
3587            var servletPath = validateServletPath(uppm.getPrefix());
3588            sb.req(new HttpServletRequestWrapper(sb.req()) {
3589               @Override
3590               public String getPathInfo() {
3591                  return pathInfo.charAt(0) == (char)0 ? null : pathInfo;
3592               }
3593               @Override
3594               public String getServletPath() {
3595                  return servletPath;
3596               }
3597            });
3598         } else {
3599               var call = sb.build();
3600               call.debug(isDebug(call)).status(SC_NOT_FOUND).finish();
3601               return;
3602            }
3603         }
3604
3605         // If this resource has child resources, try to recursively call them.
3606         var childMatch = getRestChildren().findMatch(sb);
3607         if (childMatch.isPresent()) {
3608            var uppm = childMatch.get().getPathMatch();
3609            var rc = childMatch.get().getChildContext();
3610            if (! uppm.hasEmptyVars()) {
3611               sb.pathVars(uppm.getVars());
3612               var pathInfo = opt(validatePathInfo(nullIfEmpty(urlDecode(uppm.getSuffix())))).orElse("\u0000");
3613               var servletPath = validateServletPath(sb.req().getServletPath() + uppm.getPrefix());
3614               var childRequest = new HttpServletRequestWrapper(sb.req()) {
3615                  @Override
3616                  public String getPathInfo() {
3617                     return pathInfo.charAt(0) == (char)0 ? null : pathInfo;
3618                  }
3619                  @Override
3620                  public String getServletPath() {
3621                     return servletPath;
3622                  }
3623               };
3624               rc.execute(rc.getResource(), childRequest, sb.res());
3625            } else {
3626               var call = sb.build();
3627               call.debug(isDebug(call)).status(SC_NOT_FOUND).finish();
3628            }
3629            return;
3630         }
3631
3632      } catch (Exception e) {
3633         handleError(sb.build(), convertThrowable(e));
3634      }
3635
3636      var s = sb.build();
3637
3638      try {
3639         localSession.set(s);
3640         s.debug(isDebug(s));
3641         startCall(s);
3642         s.run();
3643      } catch (Exception e) {
3644         handleError(s, convertThrowable(e));
3645      } finally {
3646         try {
3647            s.finish();
3648            endCall(s);
3649            // For mixin endpoints, dual-fire endCall on the mixin sub-context after the host's so host
3650            // hooks fire first, then the mixin's. Skip when no operation was resolved (e.g. 404 paths).
3651            var os = s.getOpSessionOrNull();
3652            if (os != null) {
3653               var opRestContext = os.getContext().getContext();
3654               if (opRestContext.isMixinContext)
3655                  opRestContext.endCall(s);
3656            }
3657         } finally {
3658            localSession.remove();
3659         }
3660      }
3661   }
3662
3663   /**
3664    * Allowed header URL parameters.

3673    */

3741   /**
3742    * Returns the call logger to use for this resource.
3743    *
3744    * <p>
3745    * The default call logger is {@link BasicCallLogger}. Override via {@link Rest#callLogger() @Rest(callLogger)}
3746    * on the resource class, by registering a {@link CallLogger} bean in the bean store, or by declaring a
3747    * {@link Bean @Bean}-annotated static method on the resource class:
3748    * <p class='bjava'>
3749    *    <ja>@Bean</ja> <jk>public static</jk> CallLogger myCallLogger(<i>&lt;args&gt;</i>) {...}
3750    * </p>
3751    *
3752    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3753    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#callLogger}

3755    * </ul>

3891    * @throws InternalServerError If no active request exists on the current thread.
3892    */
3893   public RestSession getLocalSession() {
3894      var rc = localSession.get();
3895      if (rc == null)
3896         throw new InternalServerError("No active request on current thread.");
3897      return rc;
3898   }
