3949
3950   /**
3951    * Returns the path for this resource as defined by the {@link Rest#path() @Rest(path)} annotation or
3952    * {@link Rest#path()} annotation attribute.
3953    *
3954    * <p>
3955    * If path is not specified, returns <js>""</js>.
3956    *
3957    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3958    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#path()}
3959    * </ul>
3960    *
3961    * @return The servlet path.
3962    */
3963   public String getPath() { return path; }
3964
3965   /**
3966    * Returns the resolved top-level mount paths for this resource.
3967    *
3968    * <p>
3969    * Computed once during {@link RestContext} construction by walking the runtime-override resolution chain
3970    * (highest precedence first):
3971    * <ol>
3972    *    <li><b>Programmatic</b> &mdash; {@link Builder#paths(String...)} setter (or {@link Args#paths()} record
3973    *       field). Highest precedence; an empty array (not {@code null}) explicitly clears the mount list.
3974    *    <li><b>Getter</b> &mdash; the resource's {@code getPaths()} virtual method (defined on
3975    *       {@code RestServlet} and {@code RestResource}). {@code null} return inherits; non-{@code null} (including
3976    *       empty array) wins over the annotation default.
3977    *    <li><b>Annotation default</b> &mdash; {@link Rest#paths()}, walked top-down across the resource-class
3978    *       hierarchy with the most-derived non-empty value winning. Each element is
3979    *       <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL</a>-resolved
3980    *       against the bootstrap {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.VarResolver VarResolver}, then comma-split (trim
3981    *       each piece, drop empties).  A single element like {@code "$C{health.paths}"} can therefore expand
3982    *       to multiple mount paths.
3983    * </ol>
3984    *
3985    * <p>
3986    * The resolved array is what hosting runtimes &mdash; Jetty's auto-discovery flow in
3987    * {@code JettyServerComponent}, and Spring Boot's {@code ServletRegistrationBean} wiring &mdash; should
3988    * consume when mounting the resource at the top level.
3989    *
3990    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3991    *    <li class='note'>
3992    *       Returns an empty array (not {@code null}) when none of the rungs resolve. Hosting runtimes interpret
3993    *       an empty array as &quot;no top-level mounts&quot; and surface a clear error naming the resource.
3994    *    <li class='note'>
3995    *       The scalar {@link #getPath()} accessor remains orthogonal to this method.
3996    * </ul>
3997    *
3998    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3999    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#paths()}
4000    * </ul>
4001    *
4002    * @return The resolved mount paths (never {@code null}; possibly empty).
4003    * @since 10.0.0
4004    */
4005   public String[] getPaths() { return paths; }
4006
4007   /**
4008    * Returns the path matcher for this context.
4009    *
4010    * @return The path matcher for this context.
4011    */
4012   public UrlPathMatcher getPathMatcher() { return pathMatcher; }
4013
4014   /**
4015    * Returns the explicit list of supported accept types for this resource.
4016    *
4017    * <p>
4018    * Consists of the media types for production common to all operations on this class.
4019    *
4020    * <p>
4021    * Derived from {@link Rest#produces()} across the resource annotation chain.
4022    *
4023    * @return
4024    *    An unmodifiable list of supported <c>Accept</c> header values for this resource.
4025    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4026    */
4027   public List<MediaType> getProduces() { return produces.get(); }
4028
4029   /**
4030    * Returns the resource object.
4031    *
4032    * <p>
4033    * This is the instance of the class annotated with the {@link Rest @Rest} annotation, usually
4034    * an instance of {@link RestServlet}.
4035    *
4036    * @return
4037    *    The resource object.
4038    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4039    */
4040   public Object getResource() { return resource.get(); }
4041
4042   /**
4043    * Returns the resource class type.
4044    *
4045    * @return The resource class type.
4046    */
4047   public Class<?> getResourceClass() { return resourceClass; }
4048
4049   /**
4050    * Returns the response processors registered on this resource.
4051    *
4052    * <p>
4053    * Returned in the order they're invoked by {@link #processResponse(RestOpSession)}.
4054    * The returned array is the live backing array — callers must not mutate it.
4055    *
4056    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4057    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#responseProcessors}
4058    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ResponseProcessors">Response Processors</a>
4059    * </ul>
4060    *
4061    * @return
4062    *    The response processors for this resource.
4063    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4064    */
4065   public ResponseProcessor[] getResponseProcessors() { return beanStore.getBean(ResponseProcessor[].class).orElse(null); }
4066
4067   /**
4068    * Returns the {@link RestOpArg} classes registered on this resource.
4069    *
4070    * <p>
4071    * Per-op {@link RestOpArg} instances are resolved separately at per-op setup via the bean store
4072    * (see {@link #findRestOperationArgs(Method, BeanStore)}); this getter returns the class list
4073    * that drives that resolution.
4074    *
4075    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4076    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#restOpArgs}
4077    * </ul>
4078    *
4079    * @return
4080    *    The REST-op-arg classes for this resource.
4081    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4082    */
4083   public Class<? extends RestOpArg>[] getRestOpArgs() { return restOpArgs.get(); }
4084
4085   /**
4086    * Returns the child resources associated with this servlet.
4087    *
4088    * @return
4089    *    An unmodifiable map of child resources.
4090    *    Keys are the {@link Rest#path() @Rest(path)} annotation defined on the child resource.
4091    */
4092   public RestChildren getRestChildren() { return beanStore.getBean(RestChildren.class).orElse(null); }
4093
4094   /**
4095    * Returns the REST Java methods defined in this resource.
4096    *
4097    * <p>
4098    * These are the methods annotated with the {@link RestOp @RestOp} annotation.
4099    *
4100    * @return
4101    *    An unmodifiable map of Java method names to call method objects.
4102    */
4103   public RestOperations getRestOperations() { return beanStore.getBean(RestOperations.class).orElse(null); }
4104
4105   /**
4106    * Returns the bootstrap bean store for this context.
4107    *
4108    * <p>
4109    * This is the bean store inherited from the parent resource and does not include
4110    * any beans added by this class.
4111    *
4112    * @return The bootstrap bean store for this context.
4113    */
4114   public WritableBeanStore getBootstrapBeanStore() { return bootstrapBeanStore; }
4115
4116   /**
4117    * Returns the serializers associated with this context.
4118    *
4119    * @return The serializers associated with this context.
4120    */
4121   public SerializerSet getSerializers() { return beanStore.getBean(SerializerSet.class).orElse(null); }
4122
4123   /**
4124    * Returns the servlet init parameter returned by {@link ServletConfig#getInitParameter(String)}.
4125    *
4126    * @param name The init parameter name.
4127    * @return The servlet init parameter, or <jk>null</jk> if not found.
4128    */
4129   public String getServletInitParameter(String name) {
4130      return builder.getInitParameter(name);
4131   }
4132
4133   /**
4134    * Returns the static files associated with this context.
4135    *
4136    * @return
4137    *    The static files for this resource.
4138    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4139    */
4140   public StaticFiles getStaticFiles() { return beanStore.getBean(StaticFiles.class).orElse(null); }
4141
4142   /**
4143    * Gives access to the internal statistics on this context.
4144    *
4145    * @return The context statistics.
4146    */
4147   public RestContextStats getStats() { return new RestContextStats(startTime, getMethodExecStore().getStatsByTotalTime()); }
4148
4149   /**
4150    * Returns the swagger for the REST resource.
4151    *
4152    * @param locale The locale of the swagger to return.
4153    * @return The swagger as an {@link Optional}.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
4154    */
4155   public Optional<Swagger> getSwagger(Locale locale) {
4156      Swagger s = swaggerCache.get(locale);
4157      if (s == null) {
4158         try {
4159            var provider = getSwaggerProvider();
4160            if (provider == null)
4161               return opte();
4162            s = provider.getSwagger(this, locale);
4163            if (nn(s))
4164               swaggerCache.put(locale, s);
4165         } catch (Exception e) {
4166            throw new InternalServerError(e);
4167         }
4168      }
4169      return opt(s);
4170   }
4171
4172   /**
4173    * Returns the Swagger provider used by this resource.
4174    *
4175    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4176    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#swaggerProvider()}
4177    * </ul>
4178    *
4179    * @return
4180    *    The information provider for this resource.
4181    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4182    */
4183   public SwaggerProvider getSwaggerProvider() { return beanStore.getBean(SwaggerProvider.class).orElse(null); }
4184
4185   /**
4186    * Returns the OpenAPI 3.1 document for the REST resource.
4187    *
4188    * @param locale The locale of the document to return.
4189    * @return The OpenAPI document as an {@link Optional}.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
4190    */
4191   public Optional<OpenApi> getOpenApi(Locale locale) {
4192      OpenApi o = openApiCache.get(locale);
4193      if (o == null) {
4194         try {
4195            var provider = getOpenApiProvider();
4196            if (provider == null)
4197               return opte();
4198            o = provider.getOpenApi(this, locale);
4199            if (nn(o))
4200               openApiCache.put(locale, o);
4201         } catch (Exception e) {
4202            throw new InternalServerError(e);
4203         }
4204      }
4205      return opt(o);
4206   }
4207
4208   /**
4209    * Returns the OpenAPI 3.1 provider used by this resource.
4210    *
4211    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4212    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#openApiProvider()}
4213    * </ul>
4214    *
4215    * @return
4216    *    The OpenAPI 3.1 provider for this resource.
4217    *    <br>May be <jk>null</jk>.
4218    */
4219   public OpenApiProvider getOpenApiProvider() { return beanStore.getBean(OpenApiProvider.class).orElse(null); }
4220
4221   /**
4222    * Returns the stack trace database associated with this context.
4223    *
4224    * @return
4225    *    The stack trace database for this resource.
4226    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4227    */
4228   public ThrownStore getThrownStore() { return beanStore.getBean(ThrownStore.class).orElse(null); }
4229
4230   /**
4231    * Returns the authority path of the resource.
4232    *
4233    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4234    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#uriAuthority()}
4235    * </ul>
4236    *
4237    * @return
4238    *    The authority path of this resource.
4239    *    <br>If not specified, returns the context path of the ascendant resource.
4240    */
4241   public String getUriAuthority() {
4242      return uriAuthority.get();
4243   }
4244
4245   /**
4246    * Returns the context path of the resource.
4247    *
4248    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4249    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#uriContext()}
4250    * </ul>
4251    *
4252    * @return
4253    *    The context path of this resource.
4254    *    <br>If not specified, returns the context path of the ascendant resource.
4255    */
4256   public String getUriContext() {
4257      return uriContext.get();
4258   }
4259
4260   /**
4261    * Returns the setting on how relative URIs should be interpreted as relative to.
4262    *
4263    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4264    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#uriRelativity()}
4265    * </ul>
4266    *
4267    * @return
4268    *    The URI-resolution relativity setting value.
4269    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4270    */
4271   public UriRelativity getUriRelativity() { return uriRelativity.get(); }
4272
4273   /**
4274    * Returns the setting on how relative URIs should be resolved.
4275    *
4276    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4277    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#uriResolution()}
4278    * </ul>
4279    *
4280    * @return
4281    *    The URI-resolution setting value.
4282    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4283    */
4284   public UriResolution getUriResolution() { return uriResolution.get(); }
4285
4286   /**
4287    * Returns the variable resolver for this servlet.
4288    *
4289    * <p>
4290    * Variable resolvers are used to replace variables in property values.
4291    * They can be nested arbitrarily deep.
4292    * They can also return values that themselves contain other variables.
4293    *
4294    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
4295    * <p class='bjava'>
4296    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
4297    *       messages=<js>"nls/Messages"</js>,
4298    *       properties={
4299    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"title"</js>,value=<js>"$L{title}"</js>),  <jc>// Localized variable in Messages.properties</jc>
4300    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"javaVendor"</js>,value=<js>"$S{java.vendor,Oracle}"</js>),  <jc>// System property with default value</jc>
4301    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"foo"</js>,value=<js>"bar"</js>),
4302    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"bar"</js>,value=<js>"baz"</js>),
4303    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"v1"</js>,value=<js>"$R{foo}"</js>),  <jc>// Request variable.  value="bar"</jc>
4304    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"v1"</js>,value=<js>"$R{foo,bar}"</js>),  <jc>// Request variable.  value="bar"</jc>
4305    *       }
4306    *    )
4307    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyRestResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
4308    * </p>
4309    *
4310    * <p>
4311    * A typical usage pattern involves using variables inside the {@link HtmlDocConfig @HtmlDocConfig} annotation:
4312    * <p class='bjava'>
4313    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>(<js>"/{name}/*"</js>)
4314    *    <ja>@HtmlDocConfig</ja>(
4315    *       navlinks={
4316    *          <js>"up: $R{requestParentURI}"</js>,
4317    *          <js>"api: servlet:/api"</js>,
4318    *          <js>"stats: servlet:/stats"</js>,
4319    *          <js>"editLevel: servlet:/editLevel?logger=$A{attribute.name, OFF}"</js>
4320    *       }
4321    *       header={
4322    *          <js>"&lt;h1&gt;$L{MyLocalizedPageTitle}&lt;/h1&gt;"</js>
4323    *       },
4324    *       aside={
4325    *          <js>"$F{resources/AsideText.html}"</js>
4326    *       }
4327    *    )
4328    *    <jk>public</jk> LoggerEntry getLogger(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>, <ja>@Path</ja> String <jv>name</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
4329    * </p>
4330    *
4331    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4332    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
4333    * </ul>
4334    *
4335    * @return The var resolver in use by this resource.
4336    */
4337   public VarResolver getVarResolver() { return beanStore.getBean(VarResolver.class).orElse(null); }
4338
4339   /**
4340    * Returns the bootstrap (pre-runtime) variable resolver used during context construction.
4341    *
4342    * <p>
4343    * The bootstrap resolver has the same {@link Var} catalog as {@link #getVarResolver()} but does not have
4344    * {@link Messages} or {@link Config} beans wired in — it is used to resolve annotation attribute values
4345    * (e.g. <c>@Rest(messages=...)</c>) before those beans are built. Override via
4346    * {@link Bean @Bean(name="bootstrapVarResolver")} on a static method of the resource class.
4347    *
4348    * @return The bootstrap var resolver in use by this resource.
4349    */
4350   public VarResolver getBootstrapVarResolver() { return bootstrapVarResolver.get(); }
4351
4352   /**
4353    * Returns whether it's safe to pass the HTTP content as a <js>"content"</js> GET parameter.
4354    *
4355    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4356    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#disableContentParam()}
4357    * </ul>
4358    *
4359    * @return <jk>true</jk> if setting is enabled.
4360    */
4361   public boolean isAllowContentParam() { return allowContentParam.get(); }
4362
4363   /**
4364    * Returns whether it's safe to render stack traces in HTTP responses.
4365    *
4366    * @return <jk>true</jk> if setting is enabled.
4367    */
4368   public boolean isRenderResponseStackTraces() { return renderResponseStackTraces.get(); }
4369
4370   /**
4371    * Returns whether this resource is opted into
4372    * <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807">RFC 7807</a>
4373    * {@code application/problem+json} error responses.
4374    *
4375    * <p>
4376    * When {@code true}:
4377    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
4378    *    <li>Thrown {@code BasicHttpException}s are serialized as {@code application/problem+json} via
4379    *       {@code RestContext.handleError} &mdash; regardless of the client's {@code Accept} header.
4380    *    <li>{@code @RestOp} methods that return a {@link org.apache.juneau.bean.rfc7807.Problem} (or throw a
4381    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.bean.rfc7807.ProblemException}) are serialized by
4382    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.ProblemDetailsProcessor}, but only when the client's
4383    *       {@code Accept} header matches {@code application/problem+json} (or {@code *&#47;*}).
4384    * </ul>
4385    *
4386    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4387    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#problemDetails()}
4388    * </ul>
4389    *
4390    * @return <jk>true</jk> if RFC 7807 problem-details responses are enabled on this resource.
4391    */
4392   public boolean isProblemDetails() { return problemDetails.get(); }
4393
4394   /**
4395    * Returns whether the resource opted into per-request virtual-thread dispatch (Java 21+) via
4396    * {@code @Rest(virtualThreads=true)}.
4397    *
4398    * <p>
4399    * The flag is honored only when {@link #getVirtualThreadExecutor()} returns a non-{@code null} executor; on
4400    * runtimes older than Java 21 the executor is {@code null} and the flag is logged once at context init.
4401    *
4402    * @return <jk>true</jk> if virtual-thread dispatch is configured on this resource.
4403    */
4404   public boolean isVirtualThreadsEnabled() { return virtualThreadsEnabled.get(); }
4405
4406   /**
4407    * Returns the raw resource-level observability attribute value from {@code @Rest(observability)}.
4408    *
4409    * <ul class='values'>
4410    *    <li>{@code "true"} &mdash; strict opt-in: all operations on this resource require a wired backend.
4411    *    <li>{@code "false"} &mdash; explicit opt-out: the observability block is disabled for all operations.
4412    *    <li>{@code null} / empty &mdash; default (inherit / no-op fallback) behavior.
4413    * </ul>
4414    *
4415    * <p>
4416    * Per-operation overrides from {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestOp#observability()} (and verb
4417    * annotations) take precedence over this value; they are resolved in {@link RestOpContext}.
4418    *
4419    * @return The resolved {@code @Rest(observability)} string, or {@code null} when not set.
4420    */
4421   public String getObservabilityAttribute() { return observabilityAttribute.get(); }
4422
4423   /**
4424    * Returns whether the resource explicitly opted out of observability via {@code @Rest(observability="false")}.
4425    *
4426    * @return <jk>true</jk> when the resource-level observability is disabled.
4427    */
4428   public boolean isObservabilityDisabled() {
4429      var v = observabilityAttribute.get();
4430      return v != null && v.equalsIgnoreCase("false");
4431   }
4432
4433   /**
4434    * Returns whether the server writes W3C {@code traceparent} / {@code tracestate} response headers when a
4435    * {@link TracerHook} is active on the request.
4436    *
4437    * <p>
4438    * Defaults to {@code true} (on-when-tracer); resolved from the {@code RestContext.responseTraceparent}
4439    * env-driven default. When {@code true}, a {@link TraceContextResponseProcessor} is registered in the
4440    * response-processor chain &mdash; it writes the headers only when a non-no-op {@code TracerHook} stashed a
4441    * trace context for the request, so it stays zero-cost on the no-tracer path. When {@code false}, the
4442    * processor is not registered at all.
4443    *
4444    * @return <jk>true</jk> if trace-context response-header propagation is enabled on this resource.
4445    */
4446   public boolean isResponseTraceparent() { return defaultResponseTraceparent; }
4447
4448   /**
4449    * Returns whether SLF4J MDC propagation is enabled for {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture}
4450    * completion threads on this resource.
4451    *
4452    * <p>
4453    * Defaults to {@code true}; resolved first from the programmatic {@link Builder#mdcAsyncPropagation(boolean)}
4454    * override (if set), then from the {@code RestContext.mdcAsyncPropagation} env-driven default.
4455    * When {@code true}, {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.MdcAsyncListener} wraps the
4456    * {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletionStage#whenComplete(java.util.function.BiConsumer) whenComplete} callback
4457    * registered by {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.AsyncResponseProcessor} so the completion thread sees
4458    * the request thread's MDC context. When {@code false}, no MDC work is done.
4459    *
4460    * @return <jk>true</jk> if MDC async propagation is enabled on this resource.
4461    */
4462   public boolean isMdcAsyncPropagation() {
4463      return builder.mdcAsyncPropagation != null ? builder.mdcAsyncPropagation : defaultMdcAsyncPropagation;
4464   }
4465
4466   /**
4467    * Returns the lazily-instantiated virtual-thread executor for this resource, or {@code null} when
4468    * {@code @Rest(virtualThreads=true)} is unset, the runtime is older than Java 21, or executor construction
4469    * failed (in which case a {@code WARNING} was logged at context init).
4470    *
4471    * @return The executor, or {@code null} if virtual-thread dispatch is not active for this resource.
4472    */
4473   public Executor getVirtualThreadExecutor() { return virtualThreadExecutor.get(); }
4474
4475   /**
4476    * Returns the configured async-response timeout (milliseconds) for this resource. {@code -1} indicates that no
4477    * value was supplied at the resource level — the per-op value (or {@code AsyncResponseProcessor}'s 30-second
4478    * default) applies in that case.
4479    *
4480    * @return The async timeout in milliseconds, or {@code -1} when unset.
4481    */
4482   public long getAsyncTimeoutMillis() { return asyncTimeoutMillis.get(); }
4483
4484   /**
4485    * Returns the {@link java.util.concurrent.Executor} configured for routing
4486    * {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture} completion callbacks on this resource, or
4487    * {@code null} when {@code @Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor)} is unset (natural completion thread).
4488    *
4489    * @return The executor, or {@code null} if no async-completion executor is configured.
4490    * @since 10.0.0
4491    */
4492   public Executor getAsyncCompletionExecutor() { return asyncCompletionExecutor.get(); }
4493
4494   /**
4495    * Returns whether framework beans and operation/child contexts are eagerly initialized at construction time.
4496    *
4497    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4498    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#eagerInit()}
4499    * </ul>
4500    *
4501    * @return <jk>true</jk> if eager initialization is enabled.
4502    */
4503   public boolean isEagerInit() { return eagerInit.get(); }
4504
4505   /**
4506    * Returns whether this resource's {@code @Rest(children=...)} entries are built lazily on first invocation
4507    * rather than eagerly at parent startup.
4508    *
4509    * <p>
4510    * Resolution order (highest wins):
4511    * <ol>
4512    *   <li>{@link Builder#lazyChildInit(boolean)} programmatic knob (if explicitly set).</li>
4513    *   <li>{@link Rest#lazyChildren() @Rest(lazyChildren)} annotation chain (most-derived wins).</li>
4514    *   <li>{@code RestContext.lazyChildren} env-driven default (default {@code false}).</li>
4515    * </ol>
4516    *
4517    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4518    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#lazyChildren()}
4519    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#lazyChildInit(boolean)}
4520    * </ul>
4521    *
4522    * @return <jk>true</jk> if lazy child initialization is enabled.
4523    * @since 10.0.0
4524    */
4525   public boolean isLazyChildren() {
4526      return builder.lazyChildInit != null ? builder.lazyChildInit : lazyChildrenAnnotation.get();
4527   }
4528
4529   /**
4530    * Called during servlet initialization to invoke all {@link RestPostInit} child-last methods.
4531    *
4532    * @return This object.
4533    * @throws ServletException Error occurred.
4534    */
4535   public synchronized RestContext postInit() throws ServletException {
4536      if (initialized.get())
4537         return this;
4538      var resource2 = getResource();
4539      initializeResourceContext(resource2);
4540      for (var x : postInitInvokerPair.get().invokers) {
4541         try {
4542            x.invoke(beanStore, getResource());
4543         } catch (Exception e) {
4544            throw new ServletException(unwrap(e));
4545         }
4546      }
4547      getRestChildren().postInit();
4548      return this;
4549   }
4550
4551   private void initializeResourceContext(Object resource2) {
4552      var mi = ClassInfo.of(resource2).getMethod(x -> x.hasName("setContext") && x.hasParameterTypes(RestContext.class)).orElse(null);
4553      if (nn(mi)) {
4554         try {
4555            mi.accessible().invoke(resource2, this);
4556         } catch (ExecutableException e) {
4557            throw new RuntimeException(e.unwrap());
4558         }
4559      }
4560   }
4561
4562   /**
4563    * Called during servlet initialization to invoke all {@link RestPostInit} child-first methods.
4564    *
4565    * @return This object.
4566    * @throws ServletException Error occurred.
4567    */
4568   public RestContext postInitChildFirst() throws ServletException {
4569      if (initialized.get())
4570         return this;
4571      getRestChildren().postInitChildFirst();
4572      for (var x : postInitChildFirstInvokerPair.get().invokers) {
4573         try {
4574            x.invoke(beanStore, getResource());
4575         } catch (Exception e) {
4576            throw new ServletException(unwrap(e));
4577         }
4578      }
4579      initialized.set(true);
4580      return this;
4581   }
4582
4583   private boolean isDebug(RestSession call) {
4584      return getDebugConfig().resolve(this, call.getRequest()).enabled();
4585   }
4586
4587   /**
4588    * Validates that when {@code @Rest(observability="true")} is declared on this resource, at least one real
4589    * observability backend ({@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.metrics.MetricsRecorder} or
4590    * {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.TracerHook}) is registered in the bean store.
4591    *
4592    * <p>
4593    * Called once during {@link RestContext} construction, after all {@code @Bean} method injection has run.
4594    * If the attribute is {@code "true"} and both beans resolve to their {@code NoOp} singletons, construction
4595    * fails with a {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.BasicHttpException InternalServerError} whose message precisely
4596    * identifies the missing backends.
4597    *
4598    * <p>
4599    * Per-operation {@code @RestOp(observability="true")} startup-fails are handled the same way inside
4600    * {@link RestOpContext}.
4601    *
4602    * @throws InternalServerError when {@code @Rest(observability="true")} is set but no backend is wired.
4603    */
4604   private void checkObservabilityBackendPresent() {
4605      var attr = observabilityAttribute.get();
4606      if (!"true".equalsIgnoreCase(attr))
4607         return;
4608      var recorder = beanStore.getBean(MetricsRecorder.class).orElse(null);
4609      var tracer = beanStore.getBean(TracerHook.class).orElse(null);
4610      boolean hasRecorder = recorder != null && !(recorder instanceof NoOpMetricsRecorder);
4611      boolean hasTracer = tracer != null && !(tracer instanceof NoOpTracerHook);
4612      if (!hasRecorder && !hasTracer)
4613         throw new InternalServerError(
4614            "@Rest(observability=\"true\") is set on " + getResourceClass().getSimpleName()
4615            + " but no MetricsRecorder or TracerHook @Bean is registered. "
4616            + "Either wire a backend or change observability to \"\" (inherit) or \"false\" (opt-out).");
4617   }
4618
4619   /**
4620    * Eagerly evaluates the {@link #asyncCompletionExecutor} memoizer when the annotation or programmatic
4621    * override is non-blank, ensuring "bean not found" surfaces as a startup failure.
4622    *
4623    * @throws IllegalStateException if the named executor bean is not in the bean store.
4624    */
4625   private void checkAsyncCompletionExecutorPresent() {
4626      var name = builder.asyncCompletionExecutorName != null
4627         ? builder.asyncCompletionExecutorName
4628         : mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_asyncCompletionExecutor, null);
4629      if (name != null && !name.isBlank())
4630         asyncCompletionExecutor.get(); // throws IllegalStateException if bean is missing
4631   }
4632
4633   private void initializeFrameworkBeansForRestOps() {
4634      getMarshallingContext();
4635      getEncoders();
4636      getSerializers();
4637      getParsers();
4638      getLogger();
4639      getThrownStore();
4640      getMethodExecStore();
4641      getMessages();
4642      getVarResolver();
4643      getConfig();
4644      getResponseProcessors();
4645      getCallLogger();
4646      getPartSerializer();
4647      getPartParser();
4648      getJsonSchemaGenerator();
4649      getStaticFiles();
4650      getDefaultRequestHeaders();
4651      getDefaultResponseHeaders();
4652      getDefaultRequestAttributes();
4653      getDebugEnablement();
4654      getDebugConfig();
4655      getSwaggerProvider();
4656      getOpenApiProvider();
4657   }
4658
4659   private static Set<String> newCaseInsensitiveSet(String value) {
4660      var s = new TreeSet<>(String.CASE_INSENSITIVE_ORDER) {
4661         private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
4662
4663         @Override
4664         public boolean contains(Object v) {
4665            return v != null && super.contains(v);
4666         }
4667      };
4668      StringUtils.split(value, s::add);
4669      return u(s);
4670   }
4671
4672   private LifecycleInvokerPair buildLifecycleInvokerPair(Supplier<MethodList> methods) {
4673      var ml = MethodList.of(methods.get());
4674      var inv = ml.stream().map(this::toMethodInvoker).toArray(MethodInvoker[]::new);
4675      return new LifecycleInvokerPair(ml, inv);
4676   }
4677
4678   private MethodInvoker toMethodInvoker(Method m) {
4679      return new MethodInvoker(m, getMethodExecStats(m));
4680   }
4681
4682   private static Throwable unwrap(Throwable t) {
4683      if (t instanceof InvocationTargetException t2)
4684         return t2.getTargetException();
4685      return t;
4686   }
4687
4688   /**
4689    * Method that can be subclassed to allow uncaught throwables to be treated as other types of throwables.
4690    *
4691    * <p>
4692    * The default implementation looks at the throwable class name to determine whether it can be converted to another type:
4693    *
4694    * <ul>
4695    *    <li><js>"*AccessDenied*"</js> - Converted to {@link Unauthorized}.
4696    *    <li><js>"*Empty*"</js>,<js>"*NotFound*"</js> - Converted to {@link NotFound}.
4697    * </ul>
4698    *
4699    * @param t The thrown object.
4700    * @return The converted thrown object.
4701    */
4702   public Throwable convertThrowable(Throwable t) {
4703
4704      if (t instanceof InvocationTargetException t2)
4705         t = t2.getTargetException();
4706
4707      if (t instanceof ExecutableException t2)
4708         t = t2.getTargetException();
4709
4710      if (t instanceof BasicHttpException t2)
4711         return t2;
4712
4713      var ci = ClassInfo.of(t);
4714
4715      if (ci.hasAnnotation(Response.class))
4716         return t;
4717
4718      if (ci.isAssignableTo(ParseException.class) || ci.is(InvalidDataConversionException.class))
4719         return new BadRequest(t);
4720
4721      String n = cn(t);
4722
4723      if (co(n, "AccessDenied") || co(n, "Unauthorized"))
4724         return new Unauthorized(t);
4725
4726      if (co(n, "Empty") || co(n, "NotFound"))
4727         return new NotFound(t);
4728
4729      return t;
4730   }
4731
4732   /**
4733    * Called at the end of a request to invoke all {@link RestEndCall} methods on this context.
4734    *
4735    * <p>
4736    * Fires this context's own {@code @RestEndCall} methods against its own resource instance. The host's
4737    * {@code endCall(...)} is invoked from {@link #execute(Object, HttpServletRequest, HttpServletResponse)};
4738    * for mixin-endpoint requests, that path additionally calls {@code endCall} on the mixin sub-context
4739    * after the host's so host hooks fire first, then the mixin's.
4740    *
4741    * @param session The current request.
4742    */
4743   protected void endCall(RestSession session) {
4744      for (var x : endCallInvokerPair.get().invokers) {
4745         try {
4746            x.invoke(session.getBeanStore(), getResource());
4747         } catch (Exception e) {
4748            getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, unwrap(e), () -> String.format("Error occurred invoking finish-call method '%s'.", x.getFullName()));
4749         }
4750      }
4751   }
4752
4753   /**
4754    * Finds the {@link RestOpArg} instances to handle resolving objects on the calls to the specified Java method.
4755    *
4756    * @param m The Java method being called.
4757    * @param beanStore
4758    *    The factory used for creating beans and retrieving injected beans.
4759    *    <br>Created during context bootstrap.
4760    * @return The array of resolvers.
4761    */
4762   protected RestOpArg[] findRestOperationArgs(Method m, BeanStore beanStore) {
4763
4764      var mi = MethodInfo.of(m);
4765      var params = mi.getParameters();
4766      var ra = new RestOpArg[params.size()];
4767
4768      var bs = new BasicBeanStore(beanStore);
4769      var roa = getRestOpArgs();
4770
4771      for (var i = 0; i < params.size(); i++) {
4772         var pi = params.get(i);
4773         bs.addBean(ParameterInfo.class, pi);
4774         for (var c : roa) {
4775            try {
4776               ra[i] = BeanInstantiator.of(RestOpArg.class, bs)
4777                  .type(c)
4778                  .factoryMethodNames("getInstance", "create")
4779                  .factoryAbstainOnNull()
4780                  .run();
4781               if (nn(ra[i]))
4782                  break;
4783            } catch (ExecutableException e) {
4784               throw new InternalServerError(e.unwrap(), "Could not resolve parameter {0} on method {1}.", i, mi.getNameFull());
4785            }
4786         }
4787         if (ra[i] == null)
4788            throw new InternalServerError("Could not resolve parameter {0} on method {1}.", i, mi.getNameFull());
4789      }
4790
4791      return ra;
4792   }
4793
4794   /**
4795    * Returns the time statistics gatherer for the specified method.
4796    *
4797    * @param m The method to get statistics for.
4798    * @return The cached time-stats object.
4799    */
4800   protected MethodExecStats getMethodExecStats(Method m) {
4801      return getMethodExecStore().getStats(m);
4802   }
4803
4804   /**
4805    * Returns the list of methods to invoke after the actual REST method is called.
4806    *
4807    * @return The list of methods to invoke after the actual REST method is called.
4808    */
4809   public MethodList getPostCallMethods() { return postCallMethods.get(); }
4810
4811   /**
4812    * Returns the list of methods to invoke before the actual REST method is called.
4813    *
4814    * @return The list of methods to invoke before the actual REST method is called.
4815    */
4816   public MethodList getPreCallMethods() { return preCallMethods.get(); }
4817
4818   /**
4819    * Returns the list of methods to invoke during servlet destruction.
4820    *
4821    * @return The destroy method list, never {@code null}.
4822    */
4823   public MethodList getDestroyMethods() { return destroyInvokerPair.get().methods; }
4824
4825   /**
4826    * Returns the list of methods to invoke at the end of each HTTP request.
4827    *
4828    * @return The end-call method list, never {@code null}.
4829    */
4830   public MethodList getEndCallMethods() { return endCallInvokerPair.get().methods; }
4831
4832   /**
4833    * Returns the list of methods to invoke during servlet post-initialization (child-last phase).
4834    *
4835    * @return The post-init method list, never {@code null}.
4836    */
4837   public MethodList getPostInitMethods() { return postInitInvokerPair.get().methods; }
4838
4839   /**
4840    * Returns the list of methods to invoke during servlet post-initialization (child-first phase).
4841    *
4842    * @return The post-init-child-first method list, never {@code null}.
4843    */
4844   public MethodList getPostInitChildFirstMethods() { return postInitChildFirstInvokerPair.get().methods; }
4845
4846   /**
4847    * Returns the list of methods to invoke at the start of each HTTP request.
4848    *
4849    * @return The start-call method list, never {@code null}.
4850    */
4851   public MethodList getStartCallMethods() { return startCallInvokerPair.get().methods; }
4852
4853   /**
4854    * Method for handling response errors.
4855    *
4856    * <p>
4857    * Subclasses can override this method to provide their own custom error response handling.
4858    *
4859    * @param session The rest call.
4860    * @param e The exception that occurred.
4861    * @throws IOException Can be thrown if a problem occurred trying to write to the output stream.
4862    */
4863   protected synchronized void handleError(RestSession session, Throwable e) throws IOException {
4864
4865      session.exception(e);
4866
4867      if (session.isDebug())
4868         getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, e, () -> "Error occurred during REST call.");
4869
4870      int code = 500;
4871
4872      var ci = ClassInfo.of(e);
4873      var r = ci.getAnnotations(StatusCode.class).findFirst().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).orElse(null);
4874      if (nn(r) && r.value().length > 0)
4875         code = r.value()[0];
4876
4877      var e2 = (e instanceof BasicHttpException e22 ? e22 : new BasicHttpException(code, null, e));
4878
4879      var req = session.getRequest();
4880      var res = session.getResponse();
4881
4882      Throwable t = e2.getRootCause();
4883      if (nn(t)) {
4884         Thrown t2 = Thrown.of(t);
4885         res.setHeader(t2.getName(), t2.getValue());
4886      }
4887
4888      try {
4889         var statusCode = e2.getStatusLine().getStatusCode();
4890
4891         if (e2 instanceof ValidationException ve && writeValidationErrorBody(res, ve, statusCode, isProblemDetails()))
4892            return;
4893
4894         if (isProblemDetails() && writeProblemDetailsBody(res, e2, statusCode))
4895            return;
4896
4897         res.setContentType("text/plain");
4898         res.setHeader("Content-Encoding", "identity");
4899         res.setStatus(statusCode);
4900
4901         PrintWriter w = getResponseWriter(res);
4902
4903         try (PrintWriter w2 = w) {
4904            var httpMessage = RestUtils.getHttpResponseText(statusCode);
4905            if (nn(httpMessage))
4906               w2.append("HTTP ").append(String.valueOf(statusCode)).append(": ").append(httpMessage).append("\n\n");
4907            if (isRenderResponseStackTraces())
4908               e.printStackTrace(w2);
4909            else
4910               w2.append(e2.getFullStackMessage(true));
4911         }
4912
4913      } catch (Exception e1) {
4914         req.setAttribute("Exception", e1);
4915      }
4916   }
4917
4918   /**
4919    * Writes a {@code BasicHttpException} as an {@code application/problem+json} body. Q5(A): the {@code Accept}
4920    * header is intentionally ignored on the error path so opted-in resources always emit Problem JSON for thrown
4921    * exceptions.
4922    *
4923    * @return {@code true} if the body was written (response committed). {@code false} on serialization failure so
4924    *    the caller can fall back to the legacy {@code text/plain} writer.
4925    */
4926   private static boolean writeProblemDetailsBody(HttpServletResponse res, BasicHttpException e, int statusCode) {
4927      try {
4928         var problem = ProblemAdapters.fromException(e);
4929         res.setStatus(statusCode);
4930         res.setContentType(ContentType.APPLICATION_PROBLEM_JSON.getValue());
4931         res.setHeader("Content-Encoding", "identity");
4932         var os = res.getOutputStream();
4933         JsonSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(problem, os);
4934         os.flush();
4935         return true;
4936      } catch (Exception ex) {
4937         return false;
4938      }
4939   }
4940
4941   /**
4942    * Writes a {@link ValidationException} as a structured JSON body carrying the per-field
4943    * {@link ValidationViolation} list, so client UIs can render field-level errors.
4944    *
4945    * <p>
4946    * Two shapes depending on the resource's {@code @Rest(problemDetails)} opt-in:
4947    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
4948    *    <li><b>Problem-details ON</b> &mdash; emits {@code application/problem+json} with the standard
4949    *       RFC 7807 {@code status}/{@code title}/{@code detail} members plus an {@code errors[]} extension
4950    *       array populated from {@link ValidationException#getViolations()}. Bypasses the generic
4951    *       {@link ProblemAdapters#fromException(BasicHttpException)} path because that adapter is
4952    *       intentionally narrow (no extensions) &mdash; validation needs its own per-field payload.
4953    *    <li><b>Problem-details OFF (default)</b> &mdash; emits {@code application/json} with the simple
4954    *       {@code { "status":400, "errors":[ ... ] }} envelope. Keeps the response usable from clients that
4955    *       can't parse {@code application/problem+json} without making validation responses look like a
4956    *       text/plain stack trace.
4957    * </ul>
4958    *
4959    * @return {@code true} if the body was written (response committed). {@code false} on serialization
4960    *    failure so the caller can fall back to the legacy {@code text/plain} writer.
4961    */
4962   private static boolean writeValidationErrorBody(HttpServletResponse res, ValidationException ve, int statusCode, boolean problemDetails) {
4963      try {
4964         res.setStatus(statusCode);
4965         res.setHeader("Content-Encoding", "identity");
4966         var os = res.getOutputStream();
4967         if (problemDetails) {
4968            var problem = new Problem()
4969               .setStatus(statusCode)
4970               .setTitle(ve.getStatusLine().getReasonPhrase())
4971               .setDetail(ve.getMessage())
4972               .set("errors", ve.getViolations());
4973            res.setContentType(ContentType.APPLICATION_PROBLEM_JSON.getValue());
4974            JsonSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(problem, os);
4975         } else {
4976            var envelope = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
4977            envelope.put("status", statusCode);
4978            envelope.put("errors", ve.getViolations());
4979            res.setContentType("application/json");
4980            JsonSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(envelope, os);
4981         }
4982         os.flush();
4983         return true;
4984      } catch (Exception ex) {
4985         return false;
4986      }
4987   }
4988
4989   /**
4990    * Handle the case where a matching method was not found.
4991    *
4992    * <p>
4993    * Subclasses can override this method to provide a 2nd-chance for specifying a response.
4994    * The default implementation will simply throw an exception with an appropriate message.
4995    *
4996    * @param session The HTTP call.
4997    * @throws Exception Any exception can be thrown.
4998    */
4999   void invokeRestInitMethod(MethodInfo m, Supplier<?> resource, BeanStore beanStore) throws ServletException {
5000      try {
5001         m.inject(beanStore, resource.get());
5002      } catch (Exception e) {
5003         throw servletException(e, "Exception thrown from @RestInit method {0}.{1}.", cns(m.getDeclaringClass()), m.getSignature());
5004      }
5005   }
5006
5007   private static PrintWriter getResponseWriter(HttpServletResponse res) throws IOException {
5008      try {
5009         return res.getWriter();
5010      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") IllegalStateException x) {
5011         return new PrintWriter(new OutputStreamWriter(res.getOutputStream(), UTF8));
5012      }
5013   }
5014
5015   @SuppressWarnings({
5016      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
5017   })
5018   protected void handleNotFound(RestSession session) throws Exception {
5019      var pathInfo = session.getPathInfo();
5020      var methodUC = session.getMethod();
5021      var rc = session.getStatus();
5022      var onPath = pathInfo == null ? " on no pathInfo" : (" on path '" + pathInfo + "'");
5023      if (rc == SC_NOT_FOUND)
5024         throw new NotFound("Method ''{0}'' not found on resource with matching pattern{1}.", methodUC, onPath);
5025      else if (rc == SC_PRECONDITION_FAILED)
5026         throw new PreconditionFailed("Method ''{0}'' not found on resource{1} with matching matcher.", methodUC, onPath);
5027      else if (rc == SC_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED)
5028         throw new MethodNotAllowed("Method ''{0}'' not found on resource{1}.", methodUC, onPath);
5029      else
5030         throw new ServletException("Invalid method response: " + rc, session.getException());
5031   }
5032
5033   /**
5034    * Called during a request to invoke all {@link RestPostCall} methods.
5035    *
5036    * <p>
5037    * For mixin-endpoint requests, fires the host's {@code @RestPostCall} methods first (via the host's
5038    * {@code localPostCallInvokers}), then the mixin's per-op invokers ({@link RestOpContext#getPostCallMethods()}).
5039    * Host-endpoint requests fire only the host's per-op invokers (the established path; unchanged from pre-10.0.0).
5040    *
5041    * @param session The current request.
5042    * @throws Exception If thrown from call methods.
5043    */
5044   protected void postCall(RestOpSession session) throws Exception {
5045      var opCtx = session.getContext().getContext();
5046      if (opCtx.isMixinContext && opCtx.parentContext != null)
5047         for (var m : opCtx.parentContext.localPostCallInvokers.get())
5048            m.invoke(session);
5049      for (var m : session.getContext().getPostCallMethods())
5050         m.invoke(session);
5051   }
5052
5053   /**
5054    * Called during a request to invoke all {@link RestPreCall} methods.
5055    *
5056    * <p>
5057    * For mixin-endpoint requests, fires the host's {@code @RestPreCall} methods first (via the host's
5058    * {@code localPreCallInvokers}), then the mixin's per-op invokers ({@link RestOpContext#getPreCallMethods()}).
5059    * Host-endpoint requests fire only the host's per-op invokers (the established path; unchanged from pre-10.0.0).
5060    *
5061    * @param session The current request.
5062    * @throws Exception If thrown from call methods.
5063    */
5064   protected void preCall(RestOpSession session) throws Exception {
5065      var opCtx = session.getContext().getContext();
5066      if (opCtx.isMixinContext && opCtx.parentContext != null)
5067         for (var m : opCtx.parentContext.localPreCallInvokers.get())
5068            m.invoke(session);
5069      for (var m : session.getContext().getPreCallMethods())
5070         m.invoke(session);
5071   }
5072
5073   /**
5074    * The main method for serializing POJOs passed in through the {@link RestResponse#setContent(Object)} method or
5075    * returned by the Java method.
5076    *
5077    * <p>
5078    * Subclasses may override this method if they wish to modify the way the output is rendered or support other output
5079    * formats.
5080    *
5081    * <p>
5082    * The default implementation simply iterates through the response handlers on this resource
5083    * looking for the first one whose {@link ResponseProcessor#process(RestOpSession)} method returns
5084    * <jk>true</jk>.
5085    *
5086    * @param opSession The HTTP call.
5087    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
5088    * @throws BasicHttpException Non-200 response.
5089    * @throws NotImplemented No registered response processors could handle the call.
5090    */
5091   @SuppressWarnings({
5092      "java:S127" // Loop counter i resets to -1 on RESTART
5093   })
5094   public void processResponse(RestOpSession opSession) throws IOException, BasicHttpException, NotImplemented {
5095
5096      // Loop until we find the correct processor for the POJO.
5097      int loops = 5;
5098      var rp = getResponseProcessors();
5099      for (var i = 0; i < rp.length; i++) {
5100         var j = rp[i].process(opSession);
5101         if (j == FINISHED)
5102            return;
5103         if (j == RESTART) {
5104            if (loops-- < 0)
5105               throw new InternalServerError("Too many processing loops.");
5106            i = -1;  // Start over.
5107         }
5108      }
5109
5110      var output = opSession.getResponse().getContent().orElse(null);
5111      throw new NotImplemented("No response processors found to process output of type ''{0}''", cn(output));
5112   }
5113
5114   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
5115   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
5116      return super.properties()
5117         .a(PROP_allowContentParam, isAllowContentParam())
5118         .a(PROPERTY_allowedHeaderParams, getAllowedHeaderParams())
5119         .a(PROPERTY_allowedMethodHeaders, getAllowedMethodHeaders())
5120         .a(PROPERTY_allowedMethodParams, getAllowedMethodParams())
5121         .a(PROP_beanStore, beanStore)
5122         .a(PROPERTY_clientVersionHeader, getClientVersionHeader())
5123         .a(PROP_consumes, getConsumes())
5124         .a(PROP_defaultRequestHeaders, getDefaultRequestHeaders())
5125         .a(PROP_defaultResponseHeaders, getDefaultResponseHeaders())
5126         .a(PROP_partParser, getPartParser())
5127         .a(PROP_partSerializer, getPartSerializer())
5128         .a(PROP_produces, getProduces())
5129         .a(PROPERTY_renderResponseStackTraces, isRenderResponseStackTraces())
5130         .a(PROP_responseProcessors, getResponseProcessors())
5131         .a(PROP_restOpArgs, getRestOpArgs())
5132         .a(PROP_staticFiles, getStaticFiles())
5133         .a(PROP_swaggerProvider, getSwaggerProvider())
5134         .a(PROP_openApiProvider, getOpenApiProvider())
5135         .a(PROPERTY_uriAuthority, getUriAuthority())
5136         .a(PROPERTY_uriContext, getUriContext())
5137         .a(PROPERTY_uriRelativity, getUriRelativity())
5138         .a(PROPERTY_uriResolution, getUriResolution());
5139   }
5140
5141   /**
5142    * Called at the start of a request to invoke all {@link RestStartCall} methods on this context.
5143    *
5144    * <p>
5145    * Fires this context's own {@code @RestStartCall} methods against its own resource instance. The host
5146    * version is invoked from {@link #execute(Object, HttpServletRequest, HttpServletResponse)} before the
5147    * operation is resolved; once the operation is known to belong to a mixin sub-context,
5148    * {@link RestSession#run()} additionally calls {@code startCall} on the mixin context so host hooks fire
5149    * first, then the mixin's.
5150    *
5151    * @param session The current request.
5152    * @throws BasicHttpException If thrown from call methods.
5153    */
5154   protected void startCall(RestSession session) throws BasicHttpException {
5155      for (var x : startCallInvokerPair.get().invokers) {
5156         try {
5157            x.invoke(session.getBeanStore(), getResource());
5158         } catch (IllegalAccessException | IllegalArgumentException e) {
5159            throw new InternalServerError(e, "Error occurred invoking start-call method ''{0}''.", x.getFullName());
5160         } catch (InvocationTargetException e) {
5161            var t = e.getTargetException();
5162            if (t instanceof BasicHttpException t2)
5163               throw t2;
5164            throw new InternalServerError(t);
5165         }
5166      }
5167   }
5168}
